Name: curvature_aware_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Deterministic Spectral Estimators
Short Hypothesis: By integrating curvature-aware precision routing with deterministic spectral estimators, we can achieve more stable and efficient optimization pathways in neural networks, reducing computational overhead while maintaining high precision.
Related Work: Existing methods often rely on heuristic control mechanisms and suffer from unpredictable latency. Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) addresses some of these issues by restricting optimization to a low-rank subspace, but it still lacks a curvature-aware approach. Our proposal extends SLS by incorporating curvature diagnostics to ensure more robust and stable routing.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework that integrates curvature-aware precision routing with deterministic spectral estimators to enhance the stability and efficiency of neural network optimization. By leveraging spectral diagnostics and curvature-aware routing, our approach ensures deterministic execution pathways and reduces computational overhead. We theoretically ground our framework in information-theoretic bounds and empirically validate it on benchmark tasks, demonstrating significant improvements in stability and efficiency.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Compare the stability and efficiency of our framework against Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) on mathematical reasoning tasks. Evaluation metrics include wall-clock latency, hidden-state perturbation overhead, and task accuracy.

Experiment 2: Validate the curvature-aware routing mechanism by measuring the Hessian conditioning and gradient covariance decay across different precision tiers. Evaluation metrics include condition number constraints and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds.

Experiment 3: Assess the impact of block-level precision tiering on computational overhead and deterministic execution pathways. Evaluation metrics include exact mathematical formulations and bounded perturbation models.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risk of increased initial computational overhead due to curvature diagnostics.
Limitations in scalability to larger models due to strict overhead bounds.
Dependence on accurate spectral diagnostics for effective routing.
